title: Satureja brauneana
taxonomy: Satureja brauneana is classified within the family Lamiaceae, commonly known as the mint family. This family is a large and diverse group of flowering plants that are native to many parts of the world. The genus Satureja is a small group of flowering plants that are native to the Mediterranean region. Satureja brauneana is a species within this genus and is known for its small, fragrant flowers and its use in traditional medicine. The classification of Satureja brauneana reflects its evolutionary relationships with other plants in the Lamiaceae family.
uses: Satureja brauneana, also known as a species of savory, has been traditionally used in folk medicine for various purposes. However, more research is needed to confirm its effectiveness in treating certain conditions. Traditionally, Satureja brauneana has been used to treat digestive issues and inflammation. Its essential oils, which include carvacrol and borneol, may have antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties. Further studies are required to fully understand its medicinal uses and potential benefits.

These compounds contribute to the plant's medicinal properties, including antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial effects. parts: Satureja brauneana is a species within the Lamiaceae family. The medicinal parts of Satureja brauneana, also known as Iranian summer savory, are primarily its leaves and flowers.
